subject: Science
title: Making Energy Learning Matter
subtitle: by Reducing Household Energy Consumption
challenge: Students reduce their household's energy consumption and sustain that reduction for at least 8 weeks, proving it with real billing data.
evidence: Baseline energy bills (4+ weeks before)
Action plan (max 1 page)
Weekly photo/screenshot log
Impact bills showing sustained reduction
Household voice statement
Reflection (max 500 words)
grading: Delayed (next term possible)
ai: High — effort to fabricate exceeds effort to act
phases: Prepare: Parent letters, tracking setup, adapted arrangements
Teach: Normal unit + introduce challenge in final lessons
Live It: Background portfolio building, 5-min check-ins every 2–3 weeks
Harvest: Celebrate, grade, share collective impact

subject: Science / PHE
title: Making Nutrition Learning Matter
subtitle: by Actually Changing How Your Household Eats
challenge: Students apply what they learn about nutrition to genuinely improve their household's eating habits — cooking, shopping, meal planning — with sustained evidence of healthier choices.
evidence: Baseline meal log (1 week of typical meals, photos)
Nutrition action plan created with household
Weekly meal photos + brief log entries
Sustained meal data showing change (8+ weeks)
Household voice on what changed and how it felt
Reflection connecting science to lived change
grading: Delayed (next term possible)
ai: High — meal photos with context are authentic and personal
phases: Prepare: Parent letter, discuss household food cultures sensitively, clarify we celebrate all cuisines
Teach: Normal nutrition unit + introduce challenge in final lessons
Live It: Students cook, shop, plan — weekly logs build in background
Harvest: Share recipes and stories, grade sustained change, celebrate

subject: PHE / Science
title: Making Fitness Learning Matter
subtitle: by Actually Becoming and Staying Measurably Fitter
challenge: Students apply their understanding of body systems and fitness to genuinely improve a measurable aspect of their physical fitness — and sustain it beyond the unit.
evidence: Baseline fitness assessment (chosen metric: time, distance, reps, flexibility)
Training plan grounded in the science learned
Weekly training log with data
Progress assessments at 4-week intervals
Final assessment showing sustained improvement
Reflection connecting body systems knowledge to real training experience
grading: Delayed
ai: High — physical performance data is lived and embodied
phases: Prepare: Baseline testing protocols, safety considerations, inclusive approaches for all abilities
Teach: Normal body systems/fitness unit
Live It: Students train independently, log data, reassess monthly
Harvest: Final assessments, celebrate personal bests and sustained commitment

subject: Maths / I&S
title: Making Financial Learning Matter
subtitle: by Actually Changing Your Spending and Saving Behaviour
challenge: Students apply financial literacy to genuinely change their own money habits — saving more, spending more wisely, budgeting effectively — with sustained evidence of changed behaviour.
evidence: Baseline spending/saving snapshot (2 weeks)
Financial plan with realistic personal goals
Weekly money log (pocket money, earnings, spending decisions)
Sustained data showing behaviour change (8+ weeks)
Reflection connecting maths concepts to real financial decisions
grading: Delayed
ai: High — personal financial records are timestamped and individual
phases: Prepare: Handle financial diversity sensitively, offer adapted approaches, parent letter
Teach: Normal financial literacy unit
Live It: Students track, adjust, and sustain new habits in background
Harvest: Share strategies (never amounts), celebrate discipline and growth

subject: PHE / Design
title: Making Digital Wellness Learning Matter
subtitle: by Reducing and Sustaining Healthier Screen Habits
challenge: Students apply what they learn about digital wellness to genuinely reduce their non-productive screen time and sustain healthier digital habits, evidenced by real device data.
evidence: Baseline screen time data (2 weeks, from device settings)
Digital wellness plan with specific targets
Weekly screenshots of screen time reports
Sustained data showing reduced/shifted usage (8+ weeks)
Reflection on what changed, what was hard, and what they learned about themselves
grading: Delayed
ai: High — device screen time reports are timestamped and personal
phases: Prepare: Parent letter, sensitivity around device access and autonomy
Teach: Normal digital wellness unit
Live It: Students implement changes, weekly screen time screenshots build portfolio
Harvest: Share strategies, grade sustained change, celebrate healthier habits

subject: Language & Literature
title: Making Persuasive Writing Matter
subtitle: by Achieving Real Change Through Advocacy
challenge: Students write to a real audience about a real issue they care about — and the grade comes from whether it achieved a positive response, opened a conversation, or led to change.
evidence: Sent letter/email with timestamp and recipient details
Evidence of follow-up attempts if no initial response
Response received or change documented (policy shift, published reply, meeting arranged, acknowledgement)
Reflection on the advocacy process, what persuasion techniques worked, and what they'd do differently
grading: Same term — responses typically arrive within weeks
ai: High — real correspondence with real recipients is verifiable
phases: Prepare: Identify real issues students care about, research appropriate recipients
Teach: Normal persuasive writing unit + craft real letters to real people
Live It: Letters sent, follow-ups tracked, responses collected over 4–8 weeks
Harvest: Share outcomes, grade the real-world result, celebrate every response

subject: Any Subject
title: Making Any Learning Matter
subtitle: by Teaching a Skill to Someone Who Needs It
challenge: Students teach a meaningful skill to someone — a younger child, a family member, a community member — and prove that person can now demonstrably do what they couldn't before.
evidence: Identified learner and documented their starting point
Teaching plan (what, how, when)
Documentation of teaching sessions (photos, video clips, or log)
Evidence the learner can now perform the skill (video, testimony, demonstration)
Learner's own testimony about the experience
Reflection on what made their teaching effective or what they'd change
grading: Same term — skill transfer is demonstrable quickly
ai: High — video evidence of another person's learning can't be generated
phases: Prepare: Identify willing learners, match skills to genuine needs
Teach: Build subject knowledge + introduce the teaching challenge
Live It: Students teach, document, iterate over 3–6 weeks
Harvest: Showcase learner outcomes, celebrate skill transfer

subject: Languages
title: Making Language Learning Matter
subtitle: by Building a Healthy Cross-Cultural Relationship
challenge: Students use their target language to build and maintain a genuine, respectful, healthy relationship with someone from that language community — sustained through regular communication over weeks.
evidence: Identified language partner or connection (pen pal, community member, school link)
Communication log showing regular interaction (messages, calls, meetings) over 6+ weeks
Evidence of deepening exchange (not just transactional — shared interests, mutual learning)
Partner's testimony about the relationship
Reflection on cultural understanding gained and what respectful cross-cultural connection means to them
grading: Medium — graded once sustained contact is evidenced
ai: High — a real relationship over time with a real person can't be faked
phases: Prepare: Identify partners through pen pal programmes, community groups, or sister school links
Teach: Normal language/culture unit + introduce relationship challenge
Live It: Regular communication sustained over weeks, logged and reflected on
Harvest: Share stories of connection, celebrate relationships built

subject: Maths / I&S
title: Making Data Learning Matter
subtitle: by Helping a Decision-Maker Make a Better Choice
challenge: Students use data analysis skills to help a real person, organisation, or community group make a better, more informed decision — and prove that decision was improved by their work.
evidence: Identified decision-maker and the decision they face
Data collected, cleaned, and analysed with appropriate methods
Findings communicated to the decision-maker in an accessible format
Decision-maker testimony confirming they used the analysis and how it helped
Reflection on how data literacy made a real difference
grading: Same term — the decision point creates a natural endpoint
ai: High — real decision-maker testimony about a real decision
phases: Prepare: Help students identify real decisions in school, community, or family that need data
Teach: Normal statistics/data unit + introduce the decision challenge
Live It: Students collect data, analyse, present to their decision-maker
Harvest: Decision-maker feedback collected, grade the real-world contribution

subject: Arts
title: Making Arts Learning Matter
subtitle: by Shifting Audience Understanding of a Social Issue
challenge: Students create and present a performance, exhibition, or artwork that measurably shifts their audience's understanding of, empathy for, or engagement with a social issue — for the better.
evidence: Identified social issue and target audience
Creative work developed (performance, exhibition, installation, film, music)
Pre-performance audience survey (awareness/understanding baseline)
Performance/exhibition delivered to real audience
Post-performance audience survey showing measurable shift
Reflection on artistic choices, audience response, and what they learned about art as a force for good
grading: Same term — performance happens, surveys collected, graded
ai: High — live performance with real audience survey data
phases: Prepare: Identify issues, plan audiences, design pre/post surveys
Teach: Normal arts unit + develop work for real audience and real issue
Live It: Work created, rehearsed, performed/exhibited to real audience
Harvest: Survey data analysed, grade the measured shift, celebrate impact

subject: Design
title: Making a Noise Matter
subtitle: by Creating Noise That Has Purpose
challenge: Students investigate the appropriate noise levels for an area and purpose before designing and launching a campaign that leads to those noise levels on a more regular basis
evidence: Identified space and its genuine need (interview/observation)
Design process documentation (iterations, prototyping, testing)
Finished campaign delivered to user
Feedback at 2 weeks and 6 weeks post-delivery
Usage evidence (photos of space in use, user testimony)
Reflection on the design process and what 'useful' really means
grading: Medium — first check at 2 weeks, final grade at 6 weeks
ai: High — real user testimony over time about experience in a physical space
phases: Prepare: Identify real spaces with real needs in school or community
Teach: Normal design unit on noise pollution or sound + build for the real user throughout
Live It: Product delivered, check-ins with user at 2 and 6 weeks
Harvest: User testimony collected, grade real-world usefulness, celebrate

subject: Science / I&S
title: Making Biodiversity Learning Matter
subtitle: by Increasing Species Count in a Local Area
challenge: Students take action to increase biodiversity in a specific local area — planting, creating habitats, removing invasives — and prove with survey data that species count went up and stayed up.
evidence: Baseline species survey of chosen area (standardised method)
Intervention plan (habitat creation, planting, removal, etc.)
Implementation documentation (photos, log)
Follow-up species surveys at monthly intervals
Final survey showing sustained increase compared to baseline
Reflection on ecosystems, intervention effectiveness, and what biodiversity means locally
grading: Delayed (likely next semester)
ai: High — field survey data with dated photos at a real location
phases: Prepare: Identify survey sites, establish baseline protocols, get permissions
Teach: Normal ecosystems unit + introduce biodiversity challenge
Live It: Interventions made, monthly surveys conducted and logged
Harvest: Compare baseline to final survey, celebrate biodiversity gains, share with local community

subject: Design / I&S
title: Making Inclusion Learning Matter
subtitle: by Making a Real Space More Accessible
challenge: Students identify a space that excludes someone, design and implement changes to make it more accessible, and prove — through that person's testimony — that they can now use it.
evidence: Accessibility audit of chosen space (photos, observations, barriers identified)
Interview with person currently excluded or disadvantaged by the space
Design proposal for accessibility improvements
Implementation of changes (with permissions)
User testimony confirming improved access and experience
Reflection on inclusion, design for all, and what access really means
grading: Medium — graded once changes are made and user confirms
ai: High — real physical changes to real spaces with real user testimony
phases: Prepare: Identify spaces and users, secure permissions from space owners
Teach: Normal accessibility/inclusion unit + introduce the space challenge
Live It: Audit, design, implement changes, collect user feedback
Harvest: Before/after documentation, user testimony, grade real access gained

subject: Science / Design
title: Making Sound Learning Matter
subtitle: by Making a Space Quieter and Keeping It That Way
challenge: Students apply what they learn about sound to genuinely reduce noise levels in a specific space — classroom, corridor, home room, community area — and prove the reduction lasted.
evidence: Baseline noise level measurements (dB readings over multiple days/times)
Noise source analysis and reduction plan
Intervention implemented (acoustic treatment, behaviour change, redesign)
Follow-up noise measurements showing sustained reduction
User feedback from people who use the space
Reflection on acoustics, intervention design, and wellbeing impact
grading: Medium — before/after data with 2-week sustained check
ai: High — dB meter readings are timestamped and location-specific
phases: Prepare: Identify noisy spaces, source dB meter apps, get permissions
Teach: Normal sound/acoustics unit + introduce the noise challenge
Live It: Measurements taken, interventions designed and installed, follow-up readings
Harvest: Compare data, collect user feedback, grade sustained reduction

subject: Arts / Design / I&S
title: Making Placemaking Learning Matter
subtitle: by Creating a Space People Actually Gather In
challenge: Students create or transform a space — in school or community — into somewhere people actually want to gather, and prove with evidence that people are using it regularly.
evidence: Identified underused or unwelcoming space with documentation
Community consultation (what do people want from this space?)
Design and implementation of transformation
Usage data over 4+ weeks (headcounts, photos at regular intervals)
Testimony from people who now use the space
Reflection on placemaking, community, and what makes a space feel welcoming
grading: Medium — usage data collected over 4+ weeks post-transformation
ai: High — physical transformation with usage headcounts over time
phases: Prepare: Identify spaces, consult community, secure permissions and any budget
Teach: Normal unit on community, place, or design + introduce challenge
Live It: Space transformed, usage tracked weekly with photos and headcounts
Harvest: Present the transformation story, grade sustained usage, celebrate

subject: Science / Maths
title: Making Water Learning Matter
subtitle: by Reducing Household Water Consumption
challenge: Students reduce their household's water usage through sustained behaviour change and practical interventions, evidenced by real utility data showing the reduction lasted.
evidence: Baseline water bills or meter readings (4+ weeks)
Water-saving action plan created with household
Weekly behaviour log (changes made, challenges faced)
Impact data showing sustained reduction over 8+ weeks
Household voice statement on what changed
Reflection connecting water science to lived experience
grading: Delayed (next term possible)
ai: High — utility data is timestamped and issued by provider
phases: Prepare: Parent letter, identify water data sources (bills, meters, apps)
Teach: Normal water/resources unit + introduce challenge
Live It: Changes implemented, data collected weekly in background
Harvest: Calculate litres saved individually and as class, grade impact, celebrate

subject: Science / Design / Maths
title: Making Waste Learning Matter
subtitle: by Measurably Reducing Household Food Waste
challenge: Students measure, reduce, and sustain a reduction in their household's food waste over months — proving with weighed data that less food is being thrown away.
evidence: Baseline waste audit (weighed food waste daily for 2 weeks)
Waste reduction action plan (meal planning, storage, composting, shopping habits)
Weekly waste measurement log with photos
Impact data showing sustained reduction over 8+ weeks
Household voice statement
Reflection on waste, consumption, and what sustainability means at home
grading: Delayed
ai: High — weekly weighed waste photos are personal and timestamped
phases: Prepare: Parent letter, provide or source kitchen scales, establish weighing method
Teach: Normal waste/materials unit + introduce challenge
Live It: Weekly weigh-ins, strategies adjusted, log builds in background
Harvest: Calculate kg saved, share collective impact, grade sustained reduction

subject: Science / I&S / Maths
title: Making Transport Learning Matter
subtitle: by Shifting Your Household to More Sustainable Travel
challenge: Students work with their household to shift travel habits toward more sustainable modes — walking, cycling, public transport, carpooling — and prove with sustained data that the shift lasted.
evidence: Baseline transport log (2 weeks — every journey, mode, distance)
Transport action plan identifying realistic shifts
Weekly transport log showing changed modes over 8+ weeks
Estimated emissions comparison (baseline vs impact period)
Household voice on what changed and how it felt
Reflection on transport, emissions, and sustainable choices
grading: Delayed
ai: High — daily transport logs with routes are personal and verifiable
phases: Prepare: Parent letter, provide transport log template, discuss what's realistic per household
Teach: Normal transport/emissions unit + introduce challenge
Live It: Households shift modes, weekly logs build, brief check-ins
Harvest: Calculate km shifted and emissions saved, grade sustained change, celebrate

subject: Design / Science
title: Making Materials Learning Matter
subtitle: by Repairing and Upcycling Instead of Replacing
challenge: Students commit to repairing or upcycling items instead of replacing them — and prove with a portfolio of saved items that they changed their household's relationship with stuff.
evidence: Baseline consumption snapshot (what the household typically throws away or replaces)
Repair/upcycle commitment plan
Portfolio of repaired or upcycled items with before/after photos and descriptions
Estimated waste diverted (weight or item count)
Household voice on how attitudes to 'stuff' changed
Reflection on materials, consumption, and what sustainability means in practice
grading: Medium — portfolio builds over weeks, graded once substantial
ai: High — physical before/after photos of real repaired items
phases: Prepare: Parent letter, source basic repair tools/materials if possible, connect with local repair cafes
Teach: Normal materials/sustainability unit + introduce repair challenge
Live It: Students repair, upcycle, document — portfolio grows over weeks
Harvest: Repair exhibition, calculate waste diverted, grade portfolio and sustained habit change

subject: I&S / Maths / Design
title: Making Business Learning Matter
subtitle: by Running an Ethical Enterprise That Benefits Its Community
challenge: Students create and run a real micro-business or social enterprise that is ethical, sustainable, and demonstrably benefits its community — with evidence it sustained beyond the first week.
evidence: Business plan with ethical and sustainability commitments
Evidence of operation (sales records, service logs, customer interactions)
Community benefit documented (who was helped, what changed)
Customer/community testimony
Financial records showing viability over 6+ weeks
Reflection on ethical business, sustainability, and what 'benefit' really means
grading: Medium — graded once enterprise shows sustained operation and benefit
ai: High — real customers, real transactions, real community testimony
phases: Prepare: Permissions, scope appropriate enterprises, ethical guidelines, financial oversight
Teach: Normal economics/enterprise unit + launch real businesses
Live It: Enterprises operate over 6+ weeks, data collected, community impact tracked
Harvest: Enterprise fair, share impact stories, grade sustained benefit and ethical practice
